source.note: Unit 9 Grammar 1 (Simple past negative and wh- questions, planner pp.285-287) and Grammar 2 (go + verb + -ing, planner pp.291-292). Grammar workbook answer key used for complete charts, practice sentences, and quiz material.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Simple past: wh- questions and negative
grammarPoints.rule: To form a negative statement in the simple past, use didn't + the base form of the verb. It is the same for all persons. To form wh- questions in the past, use a wh- word + did + subject + base form of the verb.
grammarPoints.pattern: subject + didn't + base verb & wh- word + did + subject + base verb

intro_examples.text: How was your weekend? It was boring. I didn't do anything special.
intro_examples.jp: 週末はどうでしたか？つまらなかったです。特に何もしませんでした。

intro_examples.text: What did you do? I went to a soccer game.
intro_examples.jp: 何をしましたか？サッカーの試合に行きました。

intro_examples.text: Did your team win? No, they didn't win. They lost.
intro_examples.jp: あなたのチームは勝ちましたか？いいえ、勝ちませんでした。負けました。

table.title: Simple Past: Negative and Questions
notes: Use didn't + the base form of the verb for all persons.
In questions, did comes before the subject, and the verb stays in its base form.

tab1_samples.text: How was your weekend? It was boring. I didn't do anything special.
tab1_samples.jp: 週末はどうでしたか？つまらなかったです。特に何もしませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: What did you do? I went to a soccer game.
tab1_samples.jp: 何をしましたか？サッカーの試合に行きました。

tab1_samples.text: Did your team win? No, they didn't win. They lost.
tab1_samples.jp: あなたのチームは勝ちましたか？いいえ、勝ちませんでした。負けました。

tab1_samples.text: We didn't go on a picnic because it was raining!
tab1_samples.jp: 雨が降っていたのでピクニックには行きませんでした！

tab1_samples.text: We went to the movies. We saw a great movie.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは映画に行きました。素晴らしい映画を見ました。

tab1_samples.text: We played basketball on Saturday. We didn't win. We lost!
tab1_samples.jp: 土曜日にバスケットボールをしました。勝ちませんでした。負けました！

tab1_samples.text: On Sunday we had lunch at home. We didn't eat out.
tab1_samples.jp: 日曜日、私たちは家で昼食を食べました。外食はしませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: Last weekend we didn't go to the beach. The weather was bad.
tab1_samples.jp: 先週末、私たちはビーチに行きませんでした。天気が悪かったです。

tab1_samples.text: Yesterday I didn't watch TV. I played video games.
tab1_samples.jp: 昨日、私はテレビを見ませんでした。テレビゲームをしました。

tab1_samples.text: I went to the game. It was great! We won!
tab1_samples.jp: 試合に行きました。素晴らしかったです！私たちは勝ちました！

rules.title: Negative past statements use didn't + base form
rules.jp_title: 過去の否定文（didn't ＋ 動詞の原形）
rules.subtitle: Always use didn't followed by the base form of the verb, never the past tense form.
rules.jp_subtitle: 過去のことを否定するときは、didn't の後ろに動詞の過去形ではなく、原形（元の形）を置きます。

transforms.from: I didn't went to the movies.
transforms.to: I didn't go to the movies.

examples.jp: 友達には会いませんでした。
彼らは博物館に行きませんでした。

rules.title: Wh- questions in the past
rules.jp_title: 過去を表す Wh 疑問文
rules.subtitle: To ask about past events, use a Wh- word (like What or How) followed by did + subject + base form of the verb.
rules.jp_subtitle: 過去の出来事について尋ねるときは、Wh疑問詞（WhatやHowなど）の後ろに、did ＋ 主語 ＋ 動詞の原形を置きます。

transforms.from: What did you did this weekend?
transforms.to: What did you do this weekend?

transforms.from: How did you went to the beach?
transforms.to: How did you go to the beach?

examples.jp: あなたの友達は何をしましたか。

rules.title: Short answers to Yes/No questions
rules.jp_title: Yes/No 疑問文に対する短い答え方
rules.subtitle: Use did or didn't for short answers to Yes/No questions in the past.
rules.jp_subtitle: 過去の Yes/No 疑問文に対して短く答えるときは、did または didn't を使います。

transforms.from: Did you stay home? Yes, I stayed.
transforms.to: Did you stay home? Yes, I did.

transforms.from: Did your team win? No, they don't.
transforms.to: Did your team win? No, they didn't.

examples.text: Did you stay home? No, I didn't.
examples.jp: 家にいましたか。いいえ、いませんでした。

examples.text: Did you see your friends? Yes, I did.
examples.jp: 友達に会いましたか。はい、会いました。

rules.title: Conjugating irregular past verbs
rules.subtitle: In positive statements, past verbs do not use -ed. Use their irregular forms (like went, won, or saw).
rules.jp_subtitle: 過去の肯定文では、不規則動詞に -ed をつけず、特別な過去形（went, won, saw など）を使います。

examples.text: I ate dinner with my friends.
examples.jp: 私は友達と一緒に夕食を食べました。
私たちは素晴らしい映画を見ました。

mixed_samples.text: Did you play basketball this weekend? No, I didn't play basketball.
mixed_samples.jp: 今週末バスケットボールをしましたか？いいえ、バスケットボールはしませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: Did you visit the museum? No, I didn't visit the museum.
mixed_samples.jp: 博物館に行きましたか？いいえ、博物館には行きませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: Did you see your friends? No, I didn't see my friends.
mixed_samples.jp: 友達に会いましたか？いいえ、友達には会いませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: Did you go to the beach? No, I didn't go to the beach. I went to the park.
mixed_samples.jp: ビーチに行きましたか？いいえ、ビーチには行きませんでした。公園に行きました。

mixed_samples.text: What did you do this weekend? I went to the museum.
mixed_samples.jp: 今週末は何をしましたか？博物館に行きました。

mixed_samples.text: How was your weekend? It was great.
mixed_samples.jp: 週末はどうでしたか？素晴らしかったです。

mixed_samples.text: Did you stay home? No, I didn't. I went to the beach.
mixed_samples.jp: 家にいましたか？いいえ、いませんでした。ビーチに行きました。

mixed_samples.text: What did you do? I played soccer.
mixed_samples.jp: 何をしましたか？サッカーをしました。

mixed_samples.text: What did your friends do? They went to the movies.
mixed_samples.jp: 友達は何をしましたか？彼らは映画に行きました。

mixed_samples.text: No, we didn't play video games. We went to the movies.
mixed_samples.jp: いいえ、私たちはテレビゲームをしませんでした。映画に行きました。

explanation.title: Past negative with didn't
explanation.body: To form the simple past negative, use 'didn't' followed by the base form of the verb: 'didn't win'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私のチームは試合に勝ちませんでした。私たちは負けました。

stem: last weekend? I went to the beach.

explanation.title: Base verb in wh- questions
explanation.body: In simple past Wh- questions, the auxiliary 'did' indicates the past tense, so the main verb must be in its base form: 'do'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 先週末は何をしましたか？ビーチに行きました。

explanation.title: Base verb in questions
explanation.body: In Yes/No past questions starting with 'Did', use the base form of the verb: 'see'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼女は友達に会いましたか？いいえ、会いませんでした。

stem: on a picnic because of the rain.

explanation.title: Base verb after didn't
explanation.body: After 'didn't', the main verb must remain in its base form: 'go'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 雨のせいで私たちはピクニックに行きませんでした。

explanation.title: Past form of 'be'
explanation.body: To ask about the state of a past event (your weekend), use the singular past form of the 'be' verb: 'was'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 週末はどうでしたか？素晴らしかったです！

stem: TV yesterday because I played video games.

explanation.title: Past negative construction
explanation.body: Use 'didn't' + base verb 'watch' to correctly say what you did not do yesterday.

tab3_quiz.jp: 昨日、私はテレビを見ませんでした。ビデオゲームをしたからです。

stem: to the movies. We saw a great comedy.

explanation.title: Irregular past of 'go'
explanation.body: In positive past sentences, use the irregular past form of 'go', which is 'went'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私たちは映画に行きました。素晴らしいコメディ映画を見ました。

stem: Did your team win? No, they

explanation.title: Short negative answer
explanation.body: To answer a past Yes/No question in the negative, use 'No, subject + didn't'.

tab3_quiz.jp: あなたのチームは勝ちましたか？いいえ、勝ちませんでした。

stem: your friends do? They went hiking.

explanation.title: Past auxiliary
explanation.body: The auxiliary verb 'did' is used to make Wh- questions in the simple past.

tab3_quiz.jp: 友達は何をしましたか？彼らはハイキングに行きました。

stem: Did you stay home? Yes, I

explanation.title: Short positive answer
explanation.body: To answer a past Yes/No question in the positive, use 'Yes, subject + did'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 家にいましたか？はい、いました。

stem: Which sentence is correct for a past negative?
answers: I didn't go to the beach.
I didn't went to the beach.
I not went to the beach.
I don't went to the beach.

explanation.title: Past negative rule
explanation.body: Use 'didn't' followed by the base verb 'go'. Never use the past form 'went' after 'didn't'.

tab4_master.jp: 私はビーチに行きませんでした。

answers: What did you do last weekend?
What do you did last weekend?
What did you did last weekend?
What did you doing last weekend?

explanation.title: Past Wh- question rule
explanation.body: The structure for Wh- questions in the past is: Wh- word + did + subject + base verb.

tab4_master.jp: 先週末は何をしましたか。

stem: How was your weekend? Choose the correct positive answer.
answers: It was great! I stayed home.
It were great! I stayed home.
It is great! I stayed home.
It did great! I stayed home.

explanation.title: Describing past weekend
explanation.body: Use the singular past form 'was' for the singular subject 'It' to describe your weekend.

tab4_master.jp: 週末はどうでしたか？素晴らしかったです！家にいました。

stem: Did your team win the match?

explanation.title: Negative past answer
explanation.body: To answer about a past negative event, use 'didn't' + base verb 'win'.

tab4_master.jp: あなたのチームは試合に勝ちましたか？

stem: What is the correct short negative answer to 'Did you stay home?'

explanation.title: Short answer format
explanation.body: In English, short answers use 'No,' + subject pronoun + 'didn't' to refer to a past question.

tab4_master.jp: 家にいましたか？

tab4_master.cue: Build the sentence about Noah's weekend.
tab4_master.jp: 私は日曜日の朝、家にいました。
私たちは昨日ピクニックに行きませんでした。

tab4_master.cue: Build the question about past action.
tab4_master.jp: 今週末は何をしましたか？

tab4_master.cue: Build the sentence about what they saw.
tab4_master.jp: 私たちは素晴らしい映画を見ました。

tab4_master.cue: Build the question about staying home.
tab4_master.jp: 土曜日に家にいましたか？

japanese.title: 単純過去形：Wh 疑問文と否定文
japanese.rule: 過去の否定文を作るには、主語に関わらず didn't ＋ 動詞の原形を使用します。Wh疑問文を作るには、Wh疑問詞 ＋ did ＋ 主語 ＋ 動詞の原形を使用します。
japanese.pattern: 主語 + didn't + 動詞の原形 & Wh疑問詞 + did + 主語 + 動詞の原形...?

examples.sentence: How was your weekend? It was boring. I didn't do anything special.
What did you do? I went to a soccer game.
Did your team win? No, they didn't win. They lost.
We didn't go on a picnic because it was raining!
We went to the movies. We saw a great movie.
We played basketball on Saturday. We didn't win. We lost!
On Sunday we had lunch at home. We didn't eat out.
Last weekend we didn't go to the beach. The weather was bad.
Yesterday I didn't watch TV. I played video games.
I went to the game. It was great! We won!
Did you play basketball this weekend? No, I didn't play basketball.
Did you visit the museum? No, I didn't visit the museum.
Did you see your friends? No, I didn't see my friends.
Did you go to the beach? No, I didn't go to the beach. I went to the park.
What did you do this weekend? I went to the museum.
How was your weekend? It was great.
Did you stay home? No, I didn't. I went to the beach.
What did you do? I played soccer.
What did your friends do? They went to the movies.
No, we didn't play video games. We went to the movies.

source.note: Workbook exercises included for positive/negative practice.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: go + verb + -ing
grammarPoints.rule: We use the pattern go + -ing verb (also called a gerund) to talk about activities we do in our free time. In the past tense, change go to went for all persons, or use didn't + go + verb-ing for negative statements.

intro_examples.text: What do you do on weekends? We usually go hiking.
intro_examples.jp: 週末は何をしますか？私たちはたいていハイキングに行きます。

intro_examples.text: What did you do last weekend? We didn't go hiking. We went swimming.
intro_examples.jp: 先週末は何をしましたか？私たちはハイキングには行きませんでした。泳ぎに行きました。

table.title: Free-time activities: go + verb + -ing
notes: Use went + -ing for positive past events.
Use didn't go + -ing (not didn't went) for negative past events.

tab1_samples.text: What do you do on weekends? We usually go hiking.
tab1_samples.jp: 週末は何をしますか？私たちはたいていハイキングに行きます。

tab1_samples.text: What did you do last weekend? We didn't go hiking. We went swimming.
tab1_samples.jp: 先週末は何をしましたか？私たちはハイキングには行きませんでした。泳ぎに行きました。

tab1_samples.text: He went ice skating last weekend.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は先週末、アイススケートに行きました。

tab1_samples.text: He didn't go horseback riding on Sunday.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は日曜日、乗馬に行きませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: He went hiking in the mountains.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は山にハイキングに行きました。

tab1_samples.text: He went swimming in the cool river.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は冷たい川に泳ぎに行きました。

tab1_samples.text: He didn't go fishing because it was raining.
tab1_samples.jp: 雨が降っていたので彼は釣りに行きませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: Naomi usually goes running by the river.
tab1_samples.jp: ナオミはいつも川のそばをランニングしに行きます。

tab1_samples.text: Last weekend, Naomi didn't go running. She went horseback riding.
tab1_samples.jp: 先週末、ナオミはランニングに行きませんでした。彼女は乗馬に行きました。

tab1_samples.text: The children usually go swimming on Saturdays. Last Saturday, they didn't go swimming.
tab1_samples.jp: 子供たちはいつも土曜日に泳ぎに行きます。先週の土曜日、彼らは泳ぎに行きませんでした。

rules.title: In positive statements, change go to went
rules.jp_title: 過去の肯定文では go を went に変える
rules.subtitle: When talking about past leisure activities, use went + verb-ing. Do not use go or goed.
rules.jp_subtitle: 過去のレジャー活動について話すときは、went ＋ 動詞-ing形を使います。go や goed は使えません。

examples.text: We went skiing in the mountains.
examples.jp: 私たちは山にスキーに行きました。

examples.text: They went fishing with their grandfather.
examples.jp: 彼らはおじいちゃんと釣りに行きました。

rules.title: In negative past, use didn't go + verb-ing
rules.jp_title: 過去の否定文では didn't go ＋ 動詞-ing形
rules.subtitle: For negative past statements, use didn't go. Do not change go to went.
rules.jp_subtitle: 過去の否定文では didn't go を使い、go を went に変えてはいけません（didn't went は間違いです）。

examples.text: We didn't go walking in the park.
examples.jp: 私たちは公園にウォーキングには行きませんでした。
彼女は乗馬には行きませんでした。

rules.title: Third person singular gets goes in the present
rules.jp_title: 三人称単数の現在形では goes
rules.subtitle: In the present tense, use goes for he, she, or it. In the past tense, went is used for all subjects.
rules.jp_subtitle: 現在形では、主語が三人称単数（he, she, itなど）のときに goes を使います。過去形（went）はすべての主語で同じです。

examples.text: Carlos usually goes fishing on Sundays.
examples.jp: カルロスは日曜日にはたいてい釣りに行きます。
母はたいていランニングに行きます。

rules.title: Never insert 'to' after go/went
rules.jp_title: go/went の後ろに to を入れない
rules.subtitle: Do not put 'to' between go/went and the verb-ing form.
rules.jp_subtitle: go/went と 動詞-ing形 の間に to を挟まないように注意しましょう。

transforms.from: I went to shopping on Saturday.
They go to hiking every summer.

examples.jp: 私たちは先週末、スケートに行きました。

examples.text: What do you do on weekends?

mixed_samples.text: I usually go hiking, but last weekend, I didn't go hiking.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は普段ハイキングに行きますが、先週末はハイキングに行きませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: My mother usually goes swimming, but last night, she didn't go swimming.
mixed_samples.jp: 母は普段泳ぎに行きますが、昨夜は泳ぎに行きませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: They usually go skateboarding in the park. Yesterday, they went bike riding.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼らはいつも公園にスケートボードに行きます。昨日は自転車に乗りに行きました。

mixed_samples.text: We went hiking in the forest last Sunday.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちは先週の日曜日、森へハイキングに行きました。

mixed_samples.text: Last weekend, Naomi went horseback riding on a farm.
mixed_samples.jp: 先週末、ナオミは農場へ乗馬に行きました。

mixed_samples.text: The children went skiing in the mountains last winter.
mixed_samples.jp: 子供たちは去年の冬、山にスキーに行きました。

mixed_samples.text: My grandmother went fishing with my grandfather.
mixed_samples.jp: 私の祖母は祖父と一緒に釣りに行きました。

mixed_samples.text: What did Inma do last weekend? She went skating.
mixed_samples.jp: インマは先週末何をしましたか？彼女はスケートに行きました。

mixed_samples.text: What does Carlos do on weekends? He usually goes fishing.
mixed_samples.jp: カルロスは週末に何をしますか？彼は普段釣りに行きます。

mixed_samples.text: What did Myra do last weekend? She went horseback riding.
mixed_samples.jp: マイラは先週末何をしましたか？彼女は乗馬に行きました。

stem: hiking, but yesterday we stayed home.

explanation.title: Present habit with 'usually'
explanation.body: With the adverb 'usually' and subject 'We', use the base present form 'go'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私たちはいつもはハイキングに行きますが、昨日は家にいました。

explanation.title: go + -ing activity
explanation.body: The verb 'go' (or 'went') is followed directly by the -ing form of the verb: 'went swimming'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 先週の日曜日、彼らは湖に泳ぎに行きました。

stem: shopping because she had no money.

explanation.title: didn't + base verb
explanation.body: In the simple past negative, use 'didn't' followed by the base form 'go', not 'went'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼女はお金がなかったので買い物に行きませんでした。

explanation.title: Third person singular present
explanation.body: For singular subject 'Carlos' in the present tense, change 'go' to 'goes'.

tab3_quiz.jp: カルロスはたいてい週末に釣りに行きます。

stem: What did Naomi do? She went

explanation.title: Compound leisure phrase
explanation.body: The complete target activity is 'go horseback riding'. In the past, it becomes 'went horseback riding'.

tab3_quiz.jp: ナオミは何をしましたか？彼女は乗馬に行きました。

stem: In the winter, we went ice

explanation.title: go + -ing pattern
explanation.body: To complete the activity 'go ice skating' in the past, use the -ing form: 'went ice skating'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 冬には、私たちは凍った川へアイススケートに行きました。

explanation.title: Past tense of 'go'
explanation.body: For a past event (yesterday), use 'went' followed by the -ing verb.

tab3_quiz.jp: 昨日、彼らは公園にスケートボードに行きました。

explanation.title: Present routine with goes + -ing
explanation.body: Use goes + verb-ing to describe a recurring leisure or errand activity like 'goes shopping'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私の祖母はたいてい土曜日の朝に買い物に行きます。

stem: We didn't went swimming. Correct: We didn't

explanation.title: Negative rule correction
explanation.body: The negative past is 'didn't go', never 'didn't went'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私たちは泳ぎに行きませんでした。（正しい文：We didn't go swimming.）

stem: He usually goes fishing, but last weekend he didn't

explanation.title: Contrast with past negative
explanation.body: In past negative clauses, use 'didn't' followed by the base form 'go'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼は普段釣りに行きますが、先週末は釣りに行きませんでした。

stem: Choose the correct past tense sentence.

explanation.title: Correct go + -ing past
explanation.body: The correct past form is subject + went + verb-ing. Do not add 'to' or use 'goed'.

tab4_master.jp: 彼らは昨日泳ぎに行きました。

stem: Choose the correct negative past sentence.

explanation.title: Correct negative past
explanation.body: To form negative past statements, use didn't + base form 'go' + verb-ing.

tab4_master.jp: 彼は釣りに行きませんでした。

stem: What does Carlos do on weekends? Choose the correct answer.

explanation.title: Carlos's routine
explanation.body: Use third person singular present form 'goes' + verb-ing for Carlos's routine.

tab4_master.jp: カルロスは週末に何をしますか？

stem: What did Inma do last weekend?

explanation.title: Past action response
explanation.body: To describe Inma's past activity, use 'went' + verb-ing.

tab4_master.jp: インマは先週末何をしましたか？

stem: Which verb form is correct after didn't?

explanation.title: Auxiliary rule
explanation.body: Always use the base verb 'go' after the past negative auxiliary 'didn't'.

tab4_master.jp: didn'tの後ろに置く正しい動詞の形はどれですか。
彼は週末にハイキングに行きました。

tab4_master.cue: Build the negative sentence about Carlos.
tab4_master.jp: 彼は乗馬に行きませんでした。

tab4_master.cue: Build the past sentence about last Saturday.
tab4_master.jp: 彼らは先週の土曜日、山へスキーに行きました。
ナオミはいつも川のそばをランニングしに行きます。
週末は何をしますか？

japanese.title: go ＋ 動詞-ing形
japanese.rule: 余暇活動やレジャーについて話すときは、go ＋ 動詞-ing形（ジェランド）のパターンを使用します。過去形では go を went に変更し、過去の否定形では didn't go ＋ 動詞-ing形を使用します。
japanese.pattern: 主語 + go/goes/went/didn't go + 動詞-ing形

examples.sentence: What do you do on weekends? We usually go hiking.
What did you do last weekend? We didn't go hiking. We went swimming.
He went ice skating last weekend.
He didn't go horseback riding on Sunday.
He went hiking in the mountains.
He went swimming in the cool river.
He didn't go fishing because it was raining.
Naomi usually goes running by the river.
Last weekend, Naomi didn't go running. She went horseback riding.
The children usually go swimming on Saturdays. Last Saturday, they didn't go swimming.
I usually go hiking, but last weekend, I didn't go hiking.
My mother usually goes swimming, but last night, she didn't go swimming.
They usually go skateboarding in the park. Yesterday, they went bike riding.
We went hiking in the forest last Sunday.
Last weekend, Naomi went horseback riding on a farm.
The children went skiing in the mountains last winter.
My grandmother went fishing with my grandfather.
What did Inma do last weekend? She went skating.
What does Carlos do on weekends? He usually goes fishing.
What did Myra do last weekend? She went horseback riding.

source.note: Workbook exercises for present vs past contrast included.
